description: When running /cleanup with parallel Phase-4 workers on the same file, edits can be clobbered by neighbouring workers' Write/Edit operations. Specifically, two workers reported successful inlining of single-use `have`s but those inlinings did not survive into the final file — a later worker on a neighbouring declaration overwrote the changes. Mitigation: either serialize Phase-4 workers (one at a time), or have each worker re-read the file immediately before its Write so its edit is on the current state. The current 'dispatch many in parallel' pattern silently loses small edits.

before_code: have hH : DiscreteTopology H := hG.of_subset Set.inter_subset_left
description: Do not use haveI / letI in Lean 4. They are a Lean 3 habit — the I stands for "inline", NOT "instance". In Lean 4, plain have : / let : with an explicit type annotation (or anonymous have : with no name) is picked up by typeclass resolution just as well, and is the preferred style. This is already documented in style-rules.md but a /cleanup worker introduced a have hH : → haveI : regression — the change was in the wrong direction. The correct simplification of have hH : T := ... (where hH is never referenced by name) is to drop the unused name, giving the anonymous have : T := ..., NOT to add the I suffix. /cleanup workers should treat haveI / letI in the diff as a defect to fix, never as an output.
